measurement: range-dependent auto-aim CORE-HIT rate (SMG normal attacks) — HARDENED SECOND READ
focusReason: solo recording — only unit on field, so all normal-attack popups are hers; no Full Burst in this run
priorRun: docs/probe-data/coreband-chisato-smg.json (first read); this file is the Fable-approved hardened re-measurement (60fps frame-stepped, CORE-HIT-text classification, >=40 normals/band, pooled near+midfar windows)

fightClockOffset.note: fight_clock = video_time - offset, from the top-right MM:SS countdown (fight t = 180 - remaining). Countdown re-read at each window; video-minus-fight offset drifts ~6s early to ~3s late (integer-second rounding + minor clock drift).

colourConvention.red+orange 'CORE HIT'+critIcon: core + crit — counts as CORE (retains the CORE HIT text)
colourConvention.classificationRule: CORE iff the popup carries the 'CORE HIT' text OR renders red/red-orange; NON-CORE = white (normal) or orange-with-crit-icon-and-no-CORE-HIT (crit). Structural 'CORE HIT' text used, not hue-vs-dark-boss.

r3CoreCritRenderCheck.result: CONFIRMED — a core+crit hit still shows the red 'CORE HIT' label; it is NOT masked into a plain orange crit.
r3CoreCritRenderCheck.evidence: In near/mid frames, 'CORE HIT' labelled popups render red (e.g. 61505) AND red-orange when also crit (e.g. 52186/40007 rendered orange-tinted but with 'CORE HIT' text and a crit icon). Plain crits (orange 33548/18185/21099) have a crit icon but NO 'CORE HIT' text and are lower value. No evidence that a core+crit ever loses the CORE HIT label -> counts are NOT biased low.

excluded: Chisato's ~472% true-damage periodic proc (big distinct popups >500k: 566125 / 849187 / 1293108)
burst-cast damage (none in this solo no-FB run)
cyan BLOCK! (boss attack blocked, not damage)
red single-digit blood-splat numbers over the CHARACTER (damage taken by Chisato, not dealt)
tiny 2-digit fragments that are occluded pieces of a larger stacked number
method: Per band: extracted full-res crosshair-region frames (crop=1100:640:780:110, zoom 2x) at 0.7s spacing so each frame is an INDEPENDENT popup stack (SMG popups live <0.6s -> no cross-frame double-count; a RATE is unbiased under independent sampling). Classified each legible body-band normal (~12-62k) as core (CORE HIT text/red) vs non-core (white/orange). Reload/black-screen frames counted as available. Boss distance visually cross-checked against band label from downscaled full frames. Near and Midfar each pool two windows to reach >=40 classified normals.

bands.bossDistance: medium (spider mech, moderate size, reticle on central core)
bands.note: Higher than first read (0.138): the hardened CORE-HIT-text detection catches red 52186/61505 cores that blend into the dark mech at medium range where the reticle sits on the core.

The two near windows disagree (0.385 vs 0.209), reproducing the first read (0.385 vs 0.219) — pose/animation-dependent core exposure adds variance beyond range. near1 shows persistent red 52186/61505 cores; near2 shows mostly white 15760 stacks with occasional cores.
Overwhelmingly white 12123 + orange 18185 crits; red 30308 cores are rare. Consistent with first read (0.069).

bands.bossDistance: intermediate (mf1) to intermediate-far (mf2, end of fight)

subWindows.label: midfar1
subWindows.note: reticle on central core; red 30308/36370 cores present. Matches first read (0.133).

subWindows.label: midfar2
subWindows.note: END OF FIGHT: ZERO cores across 38 classified normals — only orange 18185 crits + white 12123. Boss further + final-pose core not exposed.

bands.note: The two midfar windows disagree STRONGLY (0.146 vs 0.0). The 106-144 window agrees with the first read; the 176-180 end-of-fight window has no cores at all, dragging the pooled rate down to 0.076.

bossDistanceCrossCheck: Boss apparent size/distance matches the band labels: near windows = large close mech; mid = medium mech (reticle on core); midfar1 = intermediate; midfar2/far = small receded boss. Frames: cb2_bossdist_{near,far,midfar}_*.png.

monotonicity.note: Pooled band rates ARE monotone with boss distance (closer = higher core rate). Within-band heterogeneity is large though: near1 vs near2 (0.385 vs 0.209) and midfar1 vs midfar2 (0.146 vs 0.0) — driven by pose/animation and exact sub-window distance, NOT a violation of the range trend.

findings: Chisato SMG auto-aim core rate is range-dependent and, pooled, MONOTONIC with distance: near ~0.31 > mid ~0.24 > midfar ~0.08 ~= far ~0.06.
ALL bands are FAR below the sim's AUTO_CORE_RATE 0.85 constant (open-questions A9): realized SMG core rate vs this raid boss is ~0.06-0.31, not 0.85.
R3 confirmed: core+crit renders red 'CORE HIT'+crit-icon, never a bare orange crit — so classifying by the CORE HIT text does not undercount cores.

Strong within-band pose dependence: the end-of-fight midfar window (fight 176-180) produced ZERO cores in 38 normals, and the two near windows differ by ~0.18 — core exposure depends on boss animation/pose, not distance alone.

vs first read: near (0.31 vs 0.29) and far (0.06 vs 0.07) reproduce closely; mid came in higher (0.24 vs 0.14, hardened core detection) and pooled midfar lower (0.08 vs 0.13, because the new 176-180 sub-window has no cores while the 106-144 sub-window still reads ~0.15).
caveats: Counts from dense overlapping popup stacks are approximate (~+/-1 core & +/-2 total per frame). Sample sizes 45-95 classified normals/band. Trend confidence (near>mid>midfar~far) and the sub-100% magnitude are robust; absolute per-band values carry the stated Wilson CIs. Crit multiplier read cleanly as x1.5 in the far band (18185=12123x1.5).
